title: EEG data from an auditory oddball task
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from participants performing an auditory oddball task conducted at the Meditation Research Institute in Rishikesh, India. Participants completed three 13-minute sessions during which they were presented with 750 auditory stimuli consisting of standard tones (70%), oddball tones (15%), and distractor noise bursts (15%), with instructions to respond to oddball stimuli via keypad. The study was approved by local and institutional ethical review boards and provides a resource for investigating event-related potentials and cognitive processing of auditory stimuli.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during three identical 13-minute sessions. Participants were seated either on the floor or in a chair with eyes closed in a darkened room. Auditory stimuli (750 total) were presented at 1 Hz with Gaussian jitter (SD 25 ms): standard tones (500 Hz, 60 ms, 70%), oddball tones (1000 Hz, 60 ms, 15%), and distractor noise bursts (1000 Hz, 60 ms, 15%). All stimuli had 5 ms rise and fall times. Participants responded to oddball stimuli via keypad.
